title: Boundary Wire Loop Signal Not Detected
shortDescription: The Automower cannot detect the signal from the boundary wire loop, preventing it from operating.
fullDescription:
The No Loop Signal error is the most common Husqvarna Automower fault. It indicates that the robot mower's receiver cannot detect the electromagnetic signal generated by the charging station and transmitted through the boundary wire buried or pegged around the perimeter of the lawn.

Without the loop signal, the Automower cannot determine the boundaries of the mowing area and will not operate as a safety measure. The boundary wire creates a magnetic field that the mower's sensors use to know when to turn around.

The most frequent cause is a break in the boundary wire, often from accidental damage during gardening, rodent chewing, or ground movement. Loose connections at the charging station, a blown charging station fuse, or a faulty signal board can also cause this error.

causes.cause: Break in the boundary wire
Loose wire connections at the charging station
Charging station fuse blown
Charging station signal board failure
Boundary wire connector corroded

symptoms: Automower displays No Loop Signal message
Mower stays parked and will not start a mowing session
Charging station indicator light is off or blinking
Mower starts but immediately stops and returns error

fixSteps.title: Check charging station connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the wire connection terminal on the charging station. Verify that the boundary wire and guide wire connections are tight and making solid contact. Clean any corrosion from the wire ends and reconnect firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check charging station indicator
fixSteps.instruction: The charging station should have a steady green LED when the loop is intact. If it is off or flashing, the station is not sending a signal. Check the power supply connection and replace the station fuse if equipped.

fixSteps.title: Locate the wire break
fixSteps.instruction: Use an AM radio tuned to a low AM frequency and walk along the boundary wire. The wire emits a faint signal you can hear as static. Where the static stops, the break is located. Alternatively, use Husqvarna's wire detector accessory.

fixSteps.title: Repair the boundary wire
fixSteps.instruction: Once the break is found, dig up the wire at the break point. Strip both ends and reconnect using a Husqvarna original waterproof connector or a gel-filled splice connector. Bury the repaired section and test the loop signal.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot locate the wire break or if the charging station signal board has failed, a Husqvarna-authorized dealer can use professional wire tracing equipment.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower no loop signal boundary wire fix

faq.q: How do I find a break in the boundary wire?
faq.a: The cheapest method is using an AM radio tuned to a low frequency. Walk along the wire and listen for the static signal to stop — the break is at that point. Husqvarna also sells a wire detector accessory.

faq.q: Can I use regular wire to repair the boundary wire?
faq.a: Use only outdoor-rated, insulated wire of the same gauge. The splice must be waterproof. Husqvarna gel-filled connectors provide the best long-term seal.

title: Guide Wire Calibration Required
shortDescription: The Automower's guide wire signal strength is outside the calibrated range and needs recalibration.
fullDescription:
The Guide Calibration Error indicates that the Automower needs to recalibrate its guide wire following system. Guide wires help the robot navigate back to the charging station and to specific areas of the lawn efficiently. When the signal strength changes — due to wire repositioning, seasonal ground moisture changes, or electromagnetic interference — the mower needs to recalibrate.

This error commonly appears after a fresh installation, after winter storage, after any modification to the wire layout, or when a new mower is placed on an existing wire installation. It can also trigger if a section of guide wire has been damaged or if there is a new source of electromagnetic interference nearby.

Calibration is typically straightforward and can be performed from the mower's menu or the Automower Connect app.

causes.cause: Guide wire layout has been modified
Seasonal ground moisture change affecting signal
New source of electromagnetic interference nearby
Guide wire partially damaged

symptoms: Automower displays guide calibration error
Mower takes excessively long to return to charging station
Mower wanders erratically when following guide wire
Mower misses the charging station approach

fixSteps.title: Run automatic calibration
fixSteps.instruction: On the mower keypad, navigate to Settings > Installation > Guide Calibration (exact path varies by model). Select Start and allow the mower to run the calibration cycle, which takes approximately 10 to 20 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check guide wire connections
fixSteps.instruction: Before recalibrating, verify that the guide wire connections at the charging station terminal are tight and clean. Loose connections produce inconsistent signal strength.

fixSteps.title: Check for interference sources
fixSteps.instruction: Identify any new sources of electromagnetic interference: underground dog fences, new power cables, transformers, or large metal objects near the guide wire. Relocate the wire away from interference sources if possible.

fixSteps.title: Test guide wire integrity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the guide wire from the charging station and measure its resistance with a multimeter. Compare to the expected resistance based on wire length (approximately 3.3 ohms per 100m). Significantly higher resistance indicates a poor connection or partial break.

whenToCallPro: If calibration repeatedly fails or the guide wire resistance is abnormal, a dealer can perform advanced signal diagnostics.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower guide calibration error fix

faq.q: How often do I need to recalibrate the guide wire?
faq.a: Typically only after installation or wire modifications. Some owners recalibrate once per season after winter storage as ground conditions change.

faq.q: Can I skip calibration and just mow?
faq.a: The mower may refuse to follow guide wires properly, resulting in long return times and missed areas. Calibration takes only 10-20 minutes and is worth doing.

title: Wheel Motor Blocked or Stalled
shortDescription: One or both drive wheel motors are unable to rotate, often due to debris or the mower being stuck.
fullDescription:
The Wheel Motor Blocked error occurs when the Automower's drive motors detect excessive resistance preventing the wheels from turning. Each wheel has its own brushless DC motor, and the controller monitors current draw to detect stalls.

The most common cause is the mower becoming stuck on an obstacle, high grass, or uneven terrain. Grass clippings, twigs, or mud packed around the wheel axles can also create enough resistance to trigger this fault. In less common cases, the wheel motor itself may be failing mechanically.

The mower will stop immediately when this error is detected to prevent motor damage from sustained overcurrent. After clearing the obstruction, the mower can typically be restarted without further action.

causes.cause: Mower stuck on obstacle or uneven ground
Grass and debris packed around wheel axles
Wheel tread worn smooth causing loss of traction
Wheel motor gear or bearing failure

symptoms: Mower stops and displays wheel motor blocked
One wheel visibly not turning
Grinding or clicking noise from wheel area
Mower unable to climb slopes it previously handled

fixSteps.title: Clear the obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Lift the mower and check if it is stuck on a root, stone, garden edge, or depression. Place it on flat ground and test if the wheels spin freely by gently pushing the mower forward.

fixSteps.title: Clean wheel axles and housing
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the mower upside down. Remove grass clippings, mud, and debris from around both drive wheel axles and the wheel wells. Use a stiff brush to clean out compacted material.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wheel tread
fixSteps.instruction: Check the rubber tread on both drive wheels. Worn or smooth treads reduce traction, causing the motors to overwork on slopes. Replace wheels if the tread is significantly worn.

fixSteps.title: Test wheel motor function
fixSteps.instruction: From the mower menu, run the wheel motor diagnostic test (Settings > Tools > Motor Test on most models). Each wheel should spin smoothly and quietly. Grinding, clicking, or failure to spin indicates an internal motor fault.

whenToCallPro: If the wheel motor diagnostic test reveals a motor failure, replacement requires disassembly of the mower chassis and is best handled by a dealer.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower wheel motor blocked fix

faq.q: My Automower keeps getting stuck in the same spot. What should I do?
faq.a: Identify the obstruction — it may be a hidden root, depression, or soft ground. Adjust the boundary wire to exclude that area or modify the terrain. Guide wires can also help the mower avoid problem areas.

faq.q: How do I know if the wheel motor is failing versus just blocked?
faq.a: Run the built-in motor diagnostic test. If the wheel does not spin during the test with nothing blocking it, the motor is likely failing. A healthy motor will spin smoothly and quietly.

title: Mower Lifted Off the Ground
shortDescription: The lift sensors have detected that the mower has been picked up or tilted, triggering a safety stop.
fullDescription:
The Lifted error is a safety feature that immediately stops the cutting blades when the Automower detects it has been lifted off the ground. This prevents injury from exposed spinning blades. The mower uses multiple sensors to detect when it is no longer in contact with the ground surface.

While this is most often a genuine safety activation (someone picked up the mower), it can also trigger falsely. Uneven terrain that causes the mower to bounce, tall grass pushing up the chassis, or worn/dirty lift sensors can cause false activations.

If the mower has a PIN code enabled, it will require the code entry after a lift event before resuming operation. This doubles as an anti-theft measure.

causes.cause: Mower was genuinely lifted (intended safety response)
Rough or bumpy terrain causing false trigger
Lift sensors dirty or blocked by debris
Lift sensor mechanism faulty

symptoms: Blades stop immediately and mower halts
Lifted message on display
PIN code required to resume
Repeated stops on bumpy lawn sections

fixSteps.title: Place mower on flat ground
fixSteps.instruction: If the mower was intentionally lifted, place it back on the lawn and enter the PIN code if prompted. Press Start to resume mowing.

fixSteps.title: Clean lift sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the mower over and clean the lift sensor plates on the underside. These are typically small metal or plastic tabs that detect ground contact. Remove any grass buildup or mud that may prevent proper sensor function.

fixSteps.title: Adjust mowing height
fixSteps.instruction: If the error occurs frequently in tall or thick grass, raise the cutting height. Thick grass can push up on the mower body and trigger the lift sensors. Gradually lower the height over multiple mowing sessions.

fixSteps.title: Level problem terrain
fixSteps.instruction: Identify areas of the lawn where the mower bounces or tips. Fill depressions, flatten mole hills, and adjust boundary wire to exclude severely uneven areas.

whenToCallPro: If the lift sensor triggers repeatedly on flat ground with clean sensors, the sensor mechanism may be damaged and need dealer service.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower lifted error fix false trigger

faq.q: Will the blades stop if someone picks up the mower?
faq.a: Yes, instantly. The blades stop within 1-2 seconds of a lift event. This is a critical safety feature and should never be disabled.

faq.q: Why does my Automower keep saying it was lifted in the same area?
faq.a: Check that area for bumps, dips, mole activity, or thick grass clumps. The mower may be bouncing hard enough to momentarily lose ground contact and trigger the sensor.

title: Mower Excessively Tilted — Safety Stop
shortDescription: The tilt sensor has detected the mower is at an excessive angle, triggering an automatic stop.
fullDescription:
The Tilted error is triggered when the Automower's internal inclinometer detects that the mower body is at an angle exceeding the safe threshold (typically greater than 45 degrees). This safety feature stops the blades to prevent the mower from rolling or operating unsafely on excessively steep terrain.

This error commonly occurs when the mower encounters a steep slope, a sunken garden edge, or gets one wheel caught in a hole. It can also happen if the mower drives onto a raised object like a garden hose or thick root.

Most Automower models can handle slopes up to 35-45% gradient (depending on the model). If your lawn has steeper sections, the boundary wire should be adjusted to exclude them.

causes.cause: Mower encountered terrain exceeding its slope capacity
Mower drove into a hole or depression
Garden edge or object causing one-sided lift
Tilt sensor calibration drift or failure

symptoms: Mower stops and displays tilted error
Mower visibly stuck on an incline
Repeated stops near slopes or garden edges
Blades immediately stop

fixSteps.title: Move mower to flat ground
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully pick up the mower (blades will already be stopped) and place it on flat lawn. Enter PIN if prompted and press Start to resume.

fixSteps.title: Identify the problem area
fixSteps.instruction: Note where the tilt occurred. Walk the area and identify steep slopes, depressions, raised edges, or objects that caused the tilt. Mark the spot for correction.

fixSteps.title: Adjust boundary wire
fixSteps.instruction: If the tilt occurs at a steep slope, move the boundary wire to exclude that area. Maintain at least 30cm clearance from steep edges. Use the Automower Connect app to create a keep-out zone if your model supports virtual boundaries.

fixSteps.title: Level terrain if possible
fixSteps.instruction: Fill depressions, remove raised objects, and grade steep transitions to make them more gradual. Even a small amount of soil can make a borderline slope accessible to the mower.

whenToCallPro: If the tilt sensor triggers on flat ground, it may need recalibration or replacement at a service center.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower tilted error steep slope fix

faq.q: What is the maximum slope my Automower can handle?
faq.a: Standard models handle up to 35% slope, while AWD models like the 435X can handle up to 70%. Check your specific model's specifications.

faq.q: Can I make my mower handle steeper slopes?
faq.a: You cannot increase the slope capacity beyond the manufacturer's spec. For steep areas, consider the AWD 435X model or use a traditional mower for those sections.

title: Mower Is Outside the Boundary Wire Area
shortDescription: The Automower has traveled beyond the boundary wire and cannot locate itself within the working area.
fullDescription:
The Outside Working Area error appears when the Automower detects that it has crossed the boundary wire and is outside its designated mowing area. The mower is programmed to detect the boundary signal and turn around before crossing it, but various conditions can cause it to overshoot.

This typically happens when the mower is traveling at speed on wet grass (reduced traction), when the boundary wire signal is weak in a particular section, or when the wire is placed too close to an edge. The mower may also end up outside the boundary if physically moved there by a person or animal.

When this occurs, the mower stops and waits for intervention, as it cannot safely navigate back without knowing where the boundary is relative to its position.

causes.cause: Wet grass reducing traction and causing boundary overshoot
Boundary wire signal weak in a section
Boundary wire too close to lawn edge
Mower physically placed or pushed outside area

symptoms: Mower stopped outside the lawn boundary
Outside working area message displayed
Mower stuck in flower bed or neighboring property
Recurring escapes in the same location

fixSteps.title: Return mower to working area
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the Automower and place it inside the boundary wire area, preferably near the charging station. Enter the PIN if prompted and press Start.

fixSteps.title: Identify escape point
fixSteps.instruction: Check the mowing history in the Automower Connect app to see where the mower left the boundary. Walk that section of boundary wire to check for issues.

fixSteps.title: Adjust boundary wire position
fixSteps.instruction: At the escape point, move the boundary wire further inward (at least 30cm from the lawn edge). This gives the mower more room to detect the signal and turn. Secure the wire with additional stakes.

fixSteps.title: Reduce speed in problem areas
fixSteps.instruction: In the Automower settings or app, reduce the mowing speed for the area near the escape point. Some models allow zone-based speed settings. Slower speed gives the mower more time to react to the boundary.

whenToCallPro: If the mower repeatedly escapes despite wire adjustments, the boundary signal strength may need to be tested and adjusted by a dealer.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower outside working area boundary escape fix

faq.q: How far should the boundary wire be from the lawn edge?
faq.a: Husqvarna recommends approximately 30cm (12 inches) from paved edges and 35cm from flower beds. On slopes, increase the distance to allow for braking.

faq.q: Will the mower find its way back if I leave it outside the boundary?
faq.a: No. The mower cannot navigate back on its own. You must physically return it inside the boundary wire area.

title: Charging Station Communication or Power Issue
shortDescription: The Automower cannot charge properly when docked at the charging station.
fullDescription:
The Charging Station Problem error indicates a communication or electrical issue between the Automower and its charging station. When the mower docks, the charging contacts on the mower must align with the plates on the station, and both the power supply and communication link must function for charging to begin.

Dirty or oxidized charging contacts are the most common cause. Over time, grass juice, dirt, and oxidation build up on the metal contact strips on both the mower and the station, preventing good electrical contact. The power adapter, charging board, or the mower's internal charging circuit can also be at fault.

Without proper charging, the mower will eventually run out of battery and stop in the middle of the lawn.

causes.cause: Dirty or oxidized charging contacts
Charging station power adapter failed
Mower not docking correctly — misalignment
Charging station circuit board failure
Mower internal charging circuit fault

symptoms: Mower docks but battery does not charge
Charging station problem message on display
Battery level continues to decrease at station
Mower repeatedly undocks and redocks
Charging station LED shows error pattern

fixSteps.title: Clean charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Use fine sandpaper (400-grit) or a pencil eraser to clean the two metal charging strips on the front of the mower and the corresponding plates on the charging station. Wipe clean with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check power adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the power adapter LED is on. Measure the output voltage with a multimeter — it should match the spec on the adapter label. Try a different power outlet. Replace the adapter if voltage is absent or incorrect.

fixSteps.title: Verify station alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the charging station is on firm, level ground. The mower should approach straight along the guide wire and dock squarely. Adjust station position if the mower consistently docks at an angle.

fixSteps.title: Test charging manually
fixSteps.instruction: Place the mower on the station by hand, ensuring the contacts align perfectly. If it charges when manually positioned but not when self-docking, the approach path needs adjustment rather than the electrical components.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning contacts and verifying the power supply does not resolve charging, the internal charging circuit in the mower or station board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower charging station problem not charging fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the charging contacts?
faq.a: Clean them monthly during the mowing season or whenever you notice charging issues. Prevention is easier than troubleshooting.

faq.q: Can rain cause charging problems?
faq.a: The charging station is designed for outdoor use, but heavy rain pooling around the base can affect connections. Ensure the station has adequate drainage.

title: Cutting Height Adjustment Motor Blocked
shortDescription: The motorized cutting height adjustment mechanism is stuck or unable to reach the set position.
fullDescription:
The Cutting Height Blocked error indicates that the electric motor responsible for adjusting the cutting height on the Automower cannot move to the requested position. Models with motorized height adjustment (settable via the keypad or app) use a small motor and gear mechanism to raise or lower the cutting disc.

Debris, grass clippings, or dirt jamming the height adjustment mechanism is the most common cause. Over time, grass paste can dry and harden around the adjustment assembly, preventing the motor from moving. Physical damage to the gears from impact can also cause this fault.

The mower may continue to mow at whatever height the disc is currently at, but it will display the error and the height cannot be changed until the blockage is cleared.

causes.cause: Grass and debris jamming the height mechanism
Height adjustment gears worn or stripped
Height motor electrical fault

symptoms: Cutting height blocked message on display
Cutting height does not change when adjusted
Grinding noise from the cutting assembly
Mower continues at wrong cutting height

fixSteps.title: Clean the cutting assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the mower upside down. Remove the cutting disc cover plate. Clean all grass clippings, dirt, and dried grass paste from around the cutting height adjustment mechanism using a stiff brush and water.

fixSteps.title: Manually test the mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, try adjusting the cutting height from the keypad. Listen for the motor sound and watch if the cutting disc assembly moves up or down. It should move smoothly without grinding.

fixSteps.title: Lubricate the mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Apply a small amount of silicone-based lubricant to the height adjustment tracks and gear teeth. Do not use WD-40 or oil-based lubricants as they attract more grass and dirt.

whenToCallPro: If the mechanism still does not move after thorough cleaning, the internal gears or motor may need replacement by a dealer.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower cutting height blocked error fix

faq.q: Can I still mow with this error?
faq.a: Yes, the mower will typically continue mowing at the current height. However, you cannot adjust the height until the blockage is resolved.

faq.q: How often should I clean the cutting assembly?
faq.a: Clean the underside and cutting assembly at least every two weeks during the mowing season to prevent buildup.

title: Cutting Blade Motor Drawing Excessive Current
shortDescription: The blade motor current draw has exceeded the safe limit, typically due to tall grass, dull blades, or obstruction.
fullDescription:
The Blade Motor Overload error indicates that the motor driving the cutting disc and blades is drawing more current than its rated limit. The controller monitors blade motor current and will stop the blades if the load exceeds the threshold to prevent motor damage.

This most commonly occurs when the mower encounters very tall or thick grass that overwhelms the cutting capacity. Dull or damaged blades increase motor load because they require more force to cut. A foreign object like a stick or rock caught under the cutting disc can also cause an immediate overload.

After triggering this error, the mower needs the obstruction cleared or the grass height reduced before resuming. Regular blade replacement is the best preventive measure.

causes.cause: Grass too tall or thick for current blade condition
Blades dull and need replacement
Foreign object under cutting disc
Blade motor bearing worn

symptoms: Blade motor overload message
Blades stop spinning but mower may continue moving
Unusual vibration or noise from cutting area
Mower struggling in dense grass patches

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the mower upside down and inspect under the cutting disc for sticks, stones, or tangled debris. Remove any foreign objects. Spin the disc by hand — it should rotate freely.

fixSteps.title: Replace cutting blades
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the three small pivoting blades on the cutting disc. If they are dull, nicked, or bent, replace all three with new Husqvarna blades. Each blade is held by a single screw and swings freely on its mount.

fixSteps.title: Reduce grass height first
fixSteps.instruction: If the lawn has grown very tall (over 10cm), cut it with a conventional mower first, then let the Automower maintain it. The robot mower is designed for frequent light cutting, not heavy mowing.

fixSteps.title: Increase cutting height temporarily
fixSteps.instruction: Raise the Automower cutting height to the maximum setting. This reduces blade load on thick grass. Gradually lower the height over several days as the lawn gets shorter.

whenToCallPro: If fresh blades and clear cutting area still cause overload, the blade motor or its bearings may need professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower blade motor overload fix blade replacement

faq.q: How often should I replace the cutting blades?
faq.a: Husqvarna recommends replacing blades every 1-2 months during the mowing season, or whenever they appear dull or damaged. Sharp blades give a cleaner cut and reduce motor strain.

faq.q: Can I sharpen the blades instead of replacing them?
faq.a: The small pivoting blades are designed to be replaced, not sharpened. They are inexpensive and replacement is the recommended approach.

title: Battery Charge Critically Low — Mower Stopped
shortDescription: The battery charge has dropped to a critically low level, and the mower has stopped to prevent deep discharge damage.
fullDescription:
The Battery Low error occurs when the Automower's lithium-ion battery voltage drops below the minimum safe operating threshold. The mower stops to prevent deep discharge, which can permanently damage lithium-ion cells. Under normal operation, the mower should return to the charging station before reaching this level.

This error typically indicates the mower could not find its way back to the station in time — perhaps due to a guide wire issue, a very large lawn, or the mower getting stuck temporarily. It can also indicate battery degradation if the mower's run time has decreased significantly from when it was new.

A deeply discharged lithium-ion battery may take longer than normal to recover its full charge. In severe cases, the battery management system may not allow charging if the cells have dropped too low.

causes.cause: Mower unable to return to station — path blocked or guide wire issue
Working area too large for single charge cycle
Battery aging and reduced capacity
Battery cell failure

symptoms: Mower stopped in the lawn, not at charging station
Battery low message on display
Mower run time significantly shorter than when new
Mower unable to complete mowing schedule

fixSteps.title: Carry mower to charging station
fixSteps.instruction: Manually carry the mower to the charging station and place it on the contacts. Allow it to charge fully before resuming operation.

fixSteps.title: Check return path
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the guide wire and boundary wire are intact. Clean charging contacts. Run a manual return-to-station test to see if the mower can navigate back properly.

fixSteps.title: Reduce working area or schedule
fixSteps.instruction: If the lawn area is near the maximum capacity for your mower model, reduce the mowing schedule or split the lawn into zones with different schedules to ensure the mower always has enough charge to return.

fixSteps.title: Check battery health
fixSteps.instruction: In the mower settings or app, check the battery capacity indicator if available. A battery at less than 60% of original capacity should be considered for replacement. Battery capacity decreases approximately 10% per year.

whenToCallPro: If the battery will not charge after being placed on the station, or if run time is drastically reduced, professional battery testing and replacement is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower battery low not charging battery replacement

faq.q: How long does an Automower battery last?
faq.a: Typically 3 to 5 years or approximately 2,000 charge cycles. Capacity gradually decreases over time, reducing run time between charges.

faq.q: Can I replace the battery myself?
faq.a: Yes, the battery is accessible by removing the top cover (several screws). Disconnect the old battery connector and install the new one. Use only genuine Husqvarna replacement batteries.

title: Front Collision Sensor Not Responding
shortDescription: The front bumper collision sensor is reporting a permanent activation or no response, indicating a sensor malfunction.
fullDescription:
The Collision Sensor Defect error indicates that the Automower's front bumper collision detection system is malfunctioning. The mower uses a pressure-sensitive bumper (and on newer models, ultrasonic sensors) to detect collisions with objects and reverse direction.

When the collision sensor is stuck in the activated state, the mower thinks it is constantly hitting something and cannot move forward. If the sensor is not responding at all, the mower would be unable to detect obstacles and could damage itself or objects, so it stops as a safety measure.

The most common cause is debris lodged between the bumper shell and the body, keeping the bumper depressed. On older models, the microswitch behind the bumper can fail. Water intrusion can also affect the sensor circuit.

causes.cause: Debris lodged between bumper and body
Bumper microswitch stuck or failed
Water intrusion into sensor circuit
Bumper shell cracked or deformed from impact

symptoms: Collision sensor defect message
Mower will not move forward
Mower constantly reverses or spins
Bumper feels stuck or does not spring back

fixSteps.title: Clean around the bumper
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the gap between the front bumper and the mower body. Use a thin brush or compressed air to remove grass, twigs, or dirt that may be wedging the bumper. Press the bumper and verify it springs back freely.

fixSteps.title: Check bumper range of motion
fixSteps.instruction: Push the bumper inward at multiple points — center, left, and right. It should depress evenly and spring back each time. If one side sticks, focus cleaning on that area.

fixSteps.title: Dry the sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: If the mower was in heavy rain, bring it indoors and let the bumper area dry for 24 hours. Compressed air can help remove trapped water. Water on the sensor contacts can cause false readings.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the microswitch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top cover of the mower (screws underneath). Locate the collision microswitch behind the bumper. Test with a multimeter for proper switching action when the bumper is pressed. Replace if stuck or non-functional.

whenToCallPro: If the bumper shell is cracked or deformed, or if the sensor circuit board is damaged, dealer replacement is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower collision sensor defect bumper fix

faq.q: Is the collision sensor essential for safe operation?
faq.a: Yes, the collision sensor prevents the mower from driving into and damaging obstacles, fences, and garden features. Do not operate the mower with a known sensor fault.

faq.q: Can pets trigger the collision sensor?
faq.a: The bumper sensor requires physical contact to trigger. Newer models with ultrasonic sensors may detect pets at a distance and avoid them before contact.

title: GPS Signal Quality Insufficient for Navigation
shortDescription: The Automower's GPS module cannot acquire a sufficient satellite fix for GPS-assisted navigation features.
fullDescription:
The GPS Signal Poor warning appears on GPS-equipped Automower models when the satellite signal quality is too low for the navigation features to function properly. GPS-assisted navigation helps the mower track which areas have been mowed and optimize its mowing pattern for better coverage.

Poor GPS signal typically occurs under heavy tree canopy, near tall buildings, or during severe weather with heavy cloud cover. The mower can still operate without GPS using its traditional random-bounce navigation, but coverage efficiency will be reduced.

This is usually an environmental issue rather than a hardware fault. If the signal is consistently poor in your entire yard, the GPS navigation feature may provide limited benefit for your particular installation.

causes.cause: Dense tree canopy blocking satellite signals
Tall buildings or structures causing signal shadowing
Temporary atmospheric conditions
GPS module antenna fault

symptoms: GPS signal poor warning on display or app
Uneven mowing coverage patterns
Mower reverts to random navigation mode
Map view in app shows gaps or inaccuracies

fixSteps.title: Wait for conditions to improve
fixSteps.instruction: If this is a new occurrence, wait 24 hours. Atmospheric conditions, satellite maintenance, or temporary obstructions may resolve on their own.

fixSteps.title: Check for new obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Survey your yard for newly grown foliage, new structures, or temporary objects that may be blocking the sky view. Trimming overhanging branches can significantly improve GPS reception.

fixSteps.title: Clean the GPS antenna area
fixSteps.instruction: The GPS antenna is located under the top cover of the mower. Ensure the top of the mower is clean and free of heavy debris or mud that could attenuate the signal.

fixSteps.title: Adjust reliance on GPS features
fixSteps.instruction: If your yard has permanent heavy canopy, consider reducing reliance on GPS features in the app settings. The mower will fall back to traditional navigation which works without GPS but may be less efficient.

whenToCallPro: If GPS signal is poor even in open sky conditions, the GPS module or antenna may be faulty and needs dealer testing.
videoSearchQuery: Husqvarna Automower GPS signal poor navigation fix

faq.q: Does my Automower need GPS to mow?
faq.a: No. GPS improves coverage efficiency by tracking mowed areas, but the mower functions perfectly with boundary and guide wire navigation alone. GPS is an optimization feature.

faq.q: Which Automower models have GPS?
faq.a: GPS-assisted navigation is available on higher-end models like the 430X, 450X, and 435X AWD. Entry-level models like the 115H use wire-based navigation only.
